active_answer: Do not singe synthetic rope as it will melt
active_question: What does Rope365 say about singeing synthetic rope?
reason: The existing Q&A directly states the source’s material-specific warning and consequence.
support_evidence: Goal: removing the fuzzies, making the rope more smooth. This will likely burn off some of the chemicals in the rope if there are any, but not all of it. Do not singe synthetic rope as it will melt.

active_answer: conditioning rope does weaken it and will shorten its lifespan
active_question: What tradeoff does Rope365 identify when conditioning rope?
edited_answer: Conditioning can weaken rope and shorten its lifespan.
edited_question: What tradeoff does Rope365 identify when conditioning rope?
paraphrase_rationale: The revised answer turns the source’s awkward construction into a concise standalone statement without changing the tradeoff.
reason: The revised answer turns the source’s awkward construction into a concise standalone statement without changing the tradeoff.
support_evidence: Some prefer to use their rope raw or very close to it, and let the process of tying make the rope softer, others will prefer to have their rope perfect from the get-go. Everything is a compromise as the process of conditioning rope does weaken it and will shorten its lifespan.

active_answer: outside or in a well-ventilated area
active_question: Where does Rope365 say to break or polish rope because the process creates dust?
reason: The existing Q&A accurately states where Rope365 says dust-producing rope processing should occur.
support_evidence: Breaking the rope makes a lot of dust, make sure to do this outside or in a well-ventilated area. Masks, glasses and/or gloves are also recommended.
